segments.text: As Jesus was leaving the Temple, one of his disciples said to him, {
Teacher, look at these massive stones and magnificent buildings!
segments.speaker: Disciple

segments.text: You see all these great buildings?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Not one stone will be left on top of another. Everything will be torn down.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives overlooking the Temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him in private,
Tell us: When this will happen? What's the sign that all this is about to be fulfilled?
segments.speaker: Peter, James, John, and Andrew

segments.text: Jesus began telling them, {
Make sure no one deceives you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many will come in my name claiming, ‘I am the Christ.’ They will deceive many people.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be troubled when you hear of wars nearby and wars far away. These things must happen but this is not the end.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Nation will fight against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and famines too. These are the beginnings of the world's birth pains.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Watch out for yourselves! They will hand you over to the courts to be tried. You will be beaten in synagogues. Because of me you will have to st and before governors and kings, and you will be witnesses to them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The good news must first be announced in every nation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When they come to arrest you and put you on trial, don't worry about what to say. Just say what you're told at that time, because it's not you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father will betray his child. Children will turn against their parents and have them condemned to death.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You will be hated by everyone because of me, but whoever endures until the end will be saved.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when you see the ‘idolatry that causes devastation’ standing where it shouldn't be (let the reader understand), then those who are in Judea should run to the mountains.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who are on the roof—don't go back inside the house to get anything.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who are out in the fields—don't go home to get a coat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How hard it will be for those who are pregnant or nursing at that time!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Pray that this won't happen during the winter.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For these will be days of trouble like never before since the beginning of God's creation until now, and they won't ever come again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If God doesn't cut short these days, no one will survive. However, for the sake of those God has chosen, he has cut them short.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Messiah,’ or ‘Look, there he is,’ don't believe it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For false Messiahs and false prophets will appear, and they will perform miraculous signs and wonders to deceive the chosen of God, if that were possible.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Watch out! I've told you everything before it happens.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is what will happen after those troubles: ‘The sun will become dark, the moon won't shine,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they will see the Son of man as he comes on the clouds, possessing great power and glory.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He will send out the angels, and gather together all his chosen ones from wherever they are,  from the most distant part of the earth to the farthest point of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branches grow soft and send out leaves, you know that summer is near.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that it's near—right outside the door!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, this generation won't come to an end until all these things have happened.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Heaven and earth will come to an end, but my teachings will not.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one knows the day or hour when this will happen—not even the angels in heaven, not even the Son; only the Father knows.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Keep watch! Stay awake! For you don't know when this will happen.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It's like a man who went away on a journey. He left his house, and gave each of his servants the authority to do what he told them. He told the doorkeeper to stay awake.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So keep watch, because you don't know when the owner of the house is coming back. It may be in the evening, in the middle of the night, before dawn, or in the morning.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You don't want to be caught sleeping if he returns unexpectedly.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What I'm telling you, I'm telling everyone: Watch!
segments.speaker: Jesus
